parts.title: History Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a history lesson in grade [8] on [specific theme, e.g. the Industrial Revolution], with three sections focusing on causes, consequences, and source criticism, and a link to the national curriculum.
Give me five strategies for teaching source criticism in history to grade [9] students, with a practical exercise for each and a reflection on its effectiveness.
Write a short guide for introducing [specific historical event, e.g. the Second World War] to a grade 7 class, with three steps and an engaging warm-up activity.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools to teach [specific theme, e.g. the Middle Ages] in grade [8], with an activity for each and a link to digital competence in the national curriculum.
Create a list of five source-criticism tasks for a grade 9 class on [specific theme, e.g. colonialism], with a primary source for each and an analysis question.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a history lesson in grade [7] on [specific theme, e.g. the Viking Age], with three steps and a link to historical empathy.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. the Reformation] into a grade [8] class using drama, with a role-play activity for each and a reflection.

Create an assessment rubric for a history lesson in grade [9] on [specific theme, e.g. the First World War], with three knowledge requirements from the national curriculum and criteria for different levels.
Give me five ideas for teaching [specific theme, e.g. antiquity] in grade [7], with a creative activity for each and a link to historical perspectives.

Write a short template for planning a history lesson in grade [8] on [specific theme, e.g. the French Revolution], with three sections (introduction, analysis, reflection) and a source-criticism task.

Suggest three ways to collaborate with a Swedish language teacher on a cross-curricular project on [specific theme, e.g. the Swedish Age of Greatness] in grade [9], with one idea and one goal for each.
Give me an example of a lesson in grade [7] on [specific theme, e.g. the Roman Empire], with three steps focusing on processes of change and a discussion task.
Create a list of five discussion questions for a grade [8] class on [specific theme, e.g. imperialism], focusing on causes and consequences and a reflection on contemporary connections.
Suggest three ways to use primary sources to teach [specific theme, e.g. the witch trials] in grade [9], with one source and one analysis task for each.
Write a short guide for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the medieval church] in grade [7], with three steps and a link to historical empathy in the national curriculum.
Give me five ideas for making [specific theme, e.g. the World Wars] more engaging for a grade [9] class, with a practical activity for each and a rationale.
Suggest three ways to assess students' understanding of [specific theme, e.g. the Renaissance] in grade [8], with one method for each and a link to the national curriculum's knowledge requirements.
Create a plan for a history lesson in grade [7] on [specific theme, e.g. prehistory], with three sections and a closing reflection task on historical continuity.
Give me an example of a source-criticism exercise for a grade [9] class on [specific theme, e.g. the Cold War], with three steps and an analysis of the source's credibility.
Write a list of three short ways to connect [specific theme, e.g. industrialisation] to the present day in a grade [8] class, with one question for each and a link to the national curriculum.
Create a detailed plan for a project on [specific theme, e.g. human rights] for a grade [9] class, with three lessons focusing on historical examples and contemporary relevance.
Give me five strategies for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the Viking Age] in grade [7], with a practical activity for each and a link to source criticism.
Write a short guide for using film in a history lesson on [specific theme, e.g. the Second World War] in grade [9], with three steps and an analysis task.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. antiquity] with geography in a grade [8] class, with a cross-curricular activity for each and one goal.
Create a list of five creative writing tasks for a grade [7] class on [specific theme, e.g. the Middle Ages], with instructions for each and a link to historical empathy.
Give me an example of a routine for ending a history lesson on [specific theme, e.g. the French Revolution] in grade [8], with three steps and a reflection question.
Suggest three ways to use timelines to teach [specific theme, e.g. imperialism] in grade [9], with an activity for each and an analysis of processes of change.
Write an assessment task for a grade [7] class on [specific theme, e.g. prehistory], with three levels and criteria linked to the national curriculum's knowledge requirements.
Give me five ideas for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the Swedish Age of Greatness] in grade [8], with a source-based activity for each and a reflection on credibility.
Write a short template for planning a history lesson in grade [9] on [specific theme, e.g. the Cold War], with three sections and a link to contemporary perspectives.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with an art teacher on a cross-curricular theme on [specific theme, e.g. the Renaissance] in grade [8], with one idea and one goal for each.
Give me an example of a discussion on [specific theme, e.g. colonialism] for a grade [9] class, with three questions and a method for assessing participation.
Create a list of five analysis tasks for a grade [7] class on [specific theme, e.g. the Roman Empire], with a primary source for each and a source-criticism question.
Suggest three ways to use role play to teach [specific theme, e.g. the Reformation] in grade [8], with a scenario for each and a reflection on empathy.
Write a short guide for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the World Wars] in grade [9], with three steps and a link to causes and consequences in the national curriculum.
Give me five ideas for connecting [specific theme, e.g. the Industrial Revolution] to contemporary social issues in a grade [8] class, with one question for each and a rationale.
Suggest three ways to use storytelling to teach [specific theme, e.g. the witch trials] in grade [7], with a narrative activity for each and one goal.
Create a plan for a history lesson in grade [9] on [specific theme, e.g. human rights], with three sections and a discussion on historical continuity.

Create a lesson series on [specific theme, e.g. colonialism] for a grade [9] class, with three lessons exploring causes, consequences, and contemporary perspectives, and a link to the national curriculum.
